raw_text: ABAYOMI-COLE, A.B., O.B.E. (1960)—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Model Sch., Prince of Wales Sch., S.L., and Durham Univ.; M.O., S.L., 1944; S.M.O., 1955; D.D.M.S., 1959; prin. M.O., 1960.

ABBOTT, B., O.B.E. (1957).—b. 1913; ed. King Edward’s Sch. and Tynemouth High Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1946; admin. offr., cl. II, W. Nig., 1954; sec. to commn. on the public service, Nig., 1954-55; admin. offr., cl. I, 1957.

ABBOTT, B. A.—b. 1914; ed. King’s Sch., King’s Coll. and Univ. Coll., Auckland, N.Z., and Jesus Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-46; cadet, Nig., 1940; admin. offr., cl. II, 1954; i/c local govt. wing, inst. of admin., N. regn., 1954; cl. I, 1957.
ABBOTT, L.—b. 1921; ed. King’s Coll. Sch., Wimbledon; mil. serv., 1941-46, staff sgt. major; clerical offr., C.O., 1938; exec. offr., 1948; H.E.O., 1950; S.E.O., 1958; principal, 1959; S.E.O., 1960.

ABBOTT, M. J.—b. 1906; ed. King’s Sch., Canterbury; barrister. Gray’s Inn; mil. serv., 1941-45, lieut.; asst. crown solr., H.K., 1936; secon. to Ethiopian Govt. as pres., high ct., 1946; puisne judge, Nig., 1950; judge, high ct. of Lagos and Cams., 1955; fedl. justice, 1957-61.

ABBOTT, S. H.—b. 1908; ed. Antigua Gram. Sch.; apptd. agric. dept., Antigua, 1929; agric. asst., St. Kitts, 1939; farm manager, 1944; agric. offr., Virgin Is., 1946; Nevis, 1949; agric. supt., Antigua, 1955; St. Kitts, 1957.

ABELA, M. J.—b. 1926; ed. Lyceum, Malta, and Hull Univ.; teacher, Malta, 1944; apptd. civil serv. (admin.) Malta, 1945; statistician, 1957; prin. govt. statistician and electoral registr., and prin. electoral offr., 1959.
ABELL, Sir Anthony, K.C.M.G. (1952), C.M.G.—b. 1906; ed. Repton and Magdalene Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr., cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1948; gov. and c-in-c., Sarawak, 1950-59.

ABRAHAM, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. St. Stanislaus Coll., B. Guiana; cler. asst., B. Guiana, 1929; dist. offr., 1945; estab. offr., 1947; prin. asst. sec., 1953; ch. estab. offr., mem. publ. serv. comsn., 1955; Gov’s. sec. and clk., ex. co., 1960.

ABRAHAM, A. L., M.V.O. (1954), Q.P.M. (1957), C.P.M.—b. 1908; ed. Southend High Sch.; police, Pal., 1930; supt., Barb., 1938; Fiji, 1944; B. Hond., 1950; comsnr. police, Gib., 1953; chief of police, B.S.I.P., 1960.
ABRAHAM, E. E., M.B.E. (1959)—b. 1900; ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; writer, treasy., Maur., 1920; clk., govt. depts., 1928-51; chief exec. offr., educ. dept., 1951; asst. sec., sec’t, 1955-60.
ABRAHAMS, A. R.—b. 1908; ed. J’ca Coll.; apptd. J’ca, 1927; pensions offr., 1944; asst. sec., 1950; prin., 1952; perm. sec., min. of comm. and wks., 1954.

ABRAHAMS, H. A.—b. 1906; ed. Calabar High Sch., J’ca.; asst., constab., J’ca., 1923; 2nd cl. clk., constab., 1933; 1st cl. clk., med. dept., 1939; examr., accts., audit dept., 1944; acctnt., dept. of agric., 1948; senr. acctnt., 1950; admin. sec., med. dept., 1952; man., govt. savings bank, 1955; prin. asst. sec., 1955.
ABRAMS, P. D.—b. 1921; ed. Kitale Sch. and Prince of Wales Sch., Ken., and Merton Coll., Oxford; admin. cadet, Ken., 1946; dist. offr., 1948.
ACTON, H. A. G.—b. 1914; ed. Eton, and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; I.C.S., 1937-47; admin. offr., Nig., 1948; cl. II, Fed. of Nig., 1955; cl. I, 1956; perm. sec., internal affrs., 1958.
ADAM, T.—b. 1920; ed. St. Ninian's Sch., Stirling High Sch., and St. Andrews Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-45; educ. offr., W. Nig., 1949; prin. educ. offr., 1955; staff inspr., 1958-61.
ADAMS, A. B.—b. 1899; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca, London and Illinois Univs.; senr. master, tech. sch., San Fernando, Trin., 1954; prin., 1955.
ADAMS, C. W.—b. 1920; ed. Public Schs., and Melbourne Tech. Coll.; mil. serv., 1940-46; wireless offr., G. & E.I.C., 1949; chief wireless offr., 1953.
ADAMS, H. L., T.D.—b. 1910; ed. Haileybury; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Ken., 1946; sec., comrce. and industry, 1949; econ. sec., E.A.H.C., 1954; chief admin. sec., 1959.

ADAMS, J. E. M.—b. 1923; ed. Modern High Sch., B. Guiana; clk. P.O., B. Guiana, 1943; asst. inspr., labr., 1949; inspr., labr., 1953; labr. comsnr., B. Hond., 1957; dep. comsnr., labr., B. Guiana, 1960.
ADAMS, J. H.—b. 1923; ed. Bowbridge Gram. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1942-46, capt.; cadet, Tang., 1948; district offr., 1950.
ADAMS, R. K.—b. 1911; asst. registr., co-op. socs., S.L., 1939; Nig., 1946; S.L., 1950.
ADAMS, R. T.—b. 1914; ed. Lowestoft Gram. Sch., and King's Coll., London; mil. serv., 1940-46, capt.; educ. offr., Tang., 1949; senr. educ. offr., 1959.
ADAMSON, A. R.—b. 1916; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., and Portsmouth Municipal Coll.; exec. engrn., Trin., 1947; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1958; chief engrn. (roads), 1958.

ADAMSON, E.—b. 1921; ed. Bootle Gram. Sch. and L'pool Univ.; mil. serv., 1943-46; junr. scienc. offr., min. of aircraft prod., 1942; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Mal., 1946; senr. exec. engrn., P.W.D., S'pore, 1955; chief engrn., 1957; D.D.P.W., 1958-60.

ADAMSON, F. G.—b. 1915; ed. Nottingham High Sch.; solr.; mil. serv., 1939-45, capt.; asst. comsnr., lands, G.C., 1946; registr., titles, Ken., 1950; asst. registr.-gen., Tang., 1951; registr.-gen., 1957.
ADAMSON, G. A. G., M.B.E.—b. 1906; mil. serv., 1940-41; game ranger, Ken., 1939; senr. game warden, 1955-57.

ADDI, M.—b. 1917; ed. Bishop Gobat Sch., Jerusalem; posts and tels. depts., Pal., 1936-48; Pal. accts. clearance office, Cyp., 1948-49; C.O., 1949-50; asst. postmaster, Nyasa., 1950; asst. acctnt., 1952; acctnt., 1955; secon., Fed. of Rhod. and Nyasa., 1954-56; acctnt., posts and tels., dept., N. Borneo, 1957; sen. postal acctnt., P.O., H.K., 1959.

ADDISON, W. R. L., M.B.E. (1956), M.C.—b. 1919; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; cadet, Uganda, 1948; A.D.O., 1950; asst. sec., E.A.H.C., 1952; clk., cent. leg. assembly, 1951-55; asst. sec., Ken., 1957; under sec. (fin. and estab.) min. of agric., 1959.

ADEBO, S. O., C.M.G. (1959)—b. 1913; ed. St. Peter's Sch., Ake, Abeokuta Gram. Sch., and King's Coll., Lagos; cler. serv., Nig., 1933-42; admin. offr., 1942; asst. fin. sec., 1954; admin. offr., cl. I., W. Nig., 1955; perm. sec., min. of finance, 1957; jt. author Review of Nigerianisation Policy and Practice, 1953.
ADEDIPE, B. A.—b. 1912; ed St. Gregory's Coll., Lagos; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cler. serv., Nig., 1933; inspr., police, 1945; asst. supt., 1949; cr. coun., 1950; senr., 1957.

ADEMOLA, Sir A. A., Kt. (1957).—b. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; mag., Nig., 1939; puisne judge, 1949; chief justice, W. regn., 1955; chief justice, fed. sup. ct., 1958; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.
ADENIYI-JONES, A.—b. 1914; ed. C.M.S. Gram. Sch., Lagos, Cathedral Sch. and Gram. Sch., Freetown, and Manchester Univ.; M.O., Nig., 1946; W. Nig.
ADESANYA, A. A.—b. 1917; ed. Higher Coll., Yaba; asst. M.O., Nig., 1944; M.O., 1949. W. Nig.
ADESIGBIN, A. A.—b. 1909; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, Birmingham and London Univs.; barrister-at-law; mag., Nig., 1948; chief mag., 1955; W. Nig.

ADESIGBIN, Mrs. A. A.—b. 1912; ed. Girls Seminary, Lagos, King's Hse. Sch. and Froebel Educ. Inst., London, and Teachers' Coll., Columbia Univ., U.S.A.; educ. offr., Nig., 1947; prin. educ. offr., 1955; admin. offr. cl. III, 1958; W. Nig.

ADIE, J. J., Brill. Star of Zanz. (4th cl. (1947)—b. 1913; ed. Kingsland Grange Prep. Sch., Shrewsbury Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-42 (K.A.R. and O.E.T.A.); admin. offr., Zanz., 1937; asst. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 1938; Zanz., 1939; p.s. to Br. Res., 1939-40; asst. sec., 1942; senr. asst. sec. and p.s. to H.H. the Sultan, 1946; secon. C.O., 1949; sec., educ., labr. and lands, Ken., 1952; chief sec., Barb., 1957; perm. sec., Ken., 1958; author of various articles on Zanzibar.

ADJIRACKOR, T. A.—b. 1914; ed. Adisadel Coll., G.C.; police constable, G.C., 1933; corporal, 1940; sgt., 1942; sub-inspr., 1946; inspr. gr. II, 1949; asst. supt., 1950; supt., 1957; sen. supt., 1958; asst. comsnr., police, 1959. (Ghana civil service).
ADSHEAD, J. W.—b. 1923; ed. Tettenhall Coll., Staffs., Blundell's Sch., and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-47; senr. auditor, N.Nig., 1958; prin. auditor, Gam., 1960.
AERS, I. B.—b. 1921; ed. Dulwich Coll., and Sandhurst; mil. serv., 1939-48, major (desps.); cadet Tang., 1948; dist. offr., 1950.

AGG, A. D.—b. 1912; ed. S. A. Collegiate Sch., Cape Town; mil. serv., 1939-40, 2nd lieut.; arch. asst., P.W.D., N. Rhod., 1938; asst. archt., 1945; archt., 1949; asst. D.P.W. (buildings), Nyasa., 1955; ret., re-app. sen. archt., E. Nig., 1959-60.
AGIUS FERRANTE, A.—b. 1918; ed. Lyceum, and Royal Univ., Malta, and London Sch. of Econ.; cler. assst., Malta, 1937; admin. civil serv., 1939; asst. comsnr., inland rev., 1958.
AGOSTINI, C.—b. 1914; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; control offr., Trin., 1941; dep. aerodrome supt., 1946; dir. of civil aviation, and aerodrome supt., 1947.

AGOSTINI, J. E.—b. 1906; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; cler. appts., govt. depts., Trin., 1924-44; senr. clk., acctnt.-gen's dept., 1945; police (immig.), 1947; prin. offr., 1949; asst. ch. immig. offr., 1952; dep. ch. immig. offr., 1954; ch. immig. offr. (redesig.), 1958.
AHMED, M. H. C.—b. 1913; prob. labr. inspr., Maur., 1938; labr. inspr., 1940; labr. field offr., 1946; asst. labr. comsnr., 1950.

AHWAI, C.—b. 1920; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., H. Kong, and London Univ.; 2nd cl. clk., Trin., 1940; govt. shorthand writer, 1944; senr. clk., sec't, 1949; prin. offr. (sec. to public serv. comsnr.), 1950; asst. sec., 1954; fed. estab. offr., T.W.I., 1958.

AINLEY, Sir John, Kt. (1957), M.C.—b. 1906; ed. St. Bees Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; dist. mag., G.C., 1935; cr. coun., 1936; puisne judge, Uga., 1946; ch. just., E. Nig., 1955; S'wak., N. Borneo and Brunei, 1959.
AINLEY, J. M.—b. 1925; ed. Huddersfield Coll. Sch., Bridlington Gram. Sch. and Yorks. Inst. of Agric.; field offr., agric., Tang., 1949; agric. offr., 1958.
AINSWORTH, T. M.—b. 1920; ed. Hyde Gram. Sch.; admin. offr., Nig., 1948; secon., Mal., 1951-54; admin. offr., Sarawak, 1956; secon., Brunei, 1958; dist. offr., S'wak., 1959.
AITCHISON, G. L.—b. 1920; ed. Cathedral Boys Sch., Shanghai, St. Albans Sch., and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1941-46, major; admin. cadet, Nig., 1947; district offr., 1952.
AITKEN, I. H.—b. 1919; ed. Greenock High Sch. and St. Andrews Univ.; tax assessor, Ken., 1944; senr. immig. offr., 1948; dep. prin. immig. offr., 1952.

AITKEN, Maj. R.—b. 1910; ed. Gregg Comm. Coll., Ipswich; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; H.M. prison service, 1936; secon. supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1946; resig.; N.Z. prisons serv., 1947; re-appointed supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1949; dep. dir. of prisons, J'ca, 1951; dir., prisons, B. Guiana, 1955.
AITKEN, W. G.—b. 1923; ed. Glasgow High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-45, lieut.; geologist, Tang., 1947; dir., geol. surv., Nyasa., 1959.
AKENHEAD, E., T.D.—b. 1913; ed. Rugby Sch.; solicitor; mil. serv., 1939-45 major; asst. land offr., Tang., 1946; senr. asst. land offr., 1957; land offr., 1957.
AKEROYD, A.—b. 1911; ed. St. Luke's Sch. and Cockburn High Sch., Leeds; architect, N. Rhodesia, 1947; senr. architect, 1955.

AKERS, R. L., M.B.E. (1958).—b. 1912; ed. Solihull Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-42, 1944-46, capt.; asst. engr., drainage and irrig., F.M.S., 1936; senr. irrig. engr., S. Leone, 1943; engr., drainage and irrig., Mal., 1946; state engr., gr. B., 1949; chief construc. engr., drainage and irrig., Fed. of Mal., 1951; dep. dir., drainage and irrig., 1955; ag. dir., 1957.
AKIM, N. B.—b. 1924; ed. Makerere Coll., Uga.; asst. M.O., Tang., 1948; M.O., 1958.

ALAKIJA, O. B.—b. 1910; ed. C.M.S. Gram. Sch. and King's Coll., Lagos, Seaford Coll. and Guy's Hosp., London; house physician and surgeon, Nig., 1939; M.O., 1940; S.M.O. (admin.) 1952; prin. M.O., Fed. of Nig., 1957; dep. chief fed. med. advr., 1959.
ALBAN, R. G. W. E.—b. 1899; ed. Berkhamsted Sch.; mil. serv., 1918-1927; lt.-col.; Indian political serv., 1927-1947; comsnr., Kamaran I., Aden, 1952.
ALBURY, F. C. W.—b. 1904; ed. privately; wireless operator, Bah., 1918; sec., bd. of agric., 1924; senr. agric. offr., 1954; dep. dir., agric., 1957.
ALBURY, K. H. E.—b. 1920; ed. Govt. High Sch., Nassau; clk., public treasy., Bah., 1940; asst. audr., 1950; audr., 1956.
ALCINDOR, F.—b. 1909; ed. Ideal High Sch., Trin.; barrister-at-law; apptd. Trin., 1933; clk., magistracy, 1940-49; senr. clk., 1949; assessor, inland rev., 1954; magistrate, 1954.

ALCOCK, R. N., C.P.M. (1952)—b. 1912; ed. Highbury Sch., King Edward VII Nautical Sch., London, and Hendon Police Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; police cadet, Nig., 1938; asst. supt., 1941; secon. S. Leone, 1943-45; senr. asst. supt., 1947; senr. supt., police, 1950; crown coun., N. Nig., 1955-60; publ. Police Instruction Book (Hausa and English versions) (3 edns.).

ALDERDICE, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. Sydney Gram. Sch., Univ. of Sydney, and London Hosp.; mil. serv., 1940-45, lt.-col.; M.O., gr. I, Trin., 1947; med. supt., Uga., 1950; senr. specialist and med. supt., 1954.

ALDOUS, L. W.—b. 1913; ed. Stationers' Co. Sch., and L.S.E., London Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-45, capt.; F.O. (German section) control comsn. for Germany, 1946-49; devel. asst., Uga., 1952; asst. sec., 1954; senr. asst. sec., 1955; under sec., 1958.

ALDRIDGE, A. S., O.B.E. (1955), M.C., Cross of Valour (Poland).—b. 1918; ed. Durham Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; admin. offr., Cyp., 1946; cl. II, 1951; cl. I, 1953; senr. admin. offr., 1954; secon., C.O., 1955; under-sec. (internal security), Cyprus, 1955; secon., C.O., 1957; temp. principal, C.O., 1959-60.
ALEONG, R. V. S.—b. 1911; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., Faraday Hse. Elec. Engrng. Coll., London; chief electrical inspector, Trin., 1950
ALEONG, R. W.—b. 1916; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., and Royal Coll. of Science, London; asst. govt. chemist, Trin., 1952; dep. govt. chemist, 1957.

ALEXANDER, D. A. R.—b. 1920; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, and Univ. Coll., London; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; dep. clk. of courts, J'ca, 1944; mag., Turks and Caicos Is., 1947; clk. of courts, J'ca, 1949; asst. legal distmtn., 1951; legal distmtn., 1955; W. Nig., 1957.

ALEXANDER, D. R. W., M.B.E.(Mil.)—b. 1918; ed. Montrose Academy and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-46, lt. col.; admin. cadet, Nig., 1946; resig. 1947; reapp. admin. offr., gr. III, Som., 1948; gr. II, 1948; secon. Cyrenaica, 1948-53; gr. I, chief sec., min. of interior, 1951; cadet offr. cl. II, H. K., 1953; admin. offr., staff gr. C, 1959.
ALEXANDER, J.—b. 1909; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Sch. and Aberdeen Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-45, W.S./capt., R.E.; engrnr., H. K., 1949; asst. chief engrnr., 1957; chief engrnr., 1958.

ALEXANDER, J. B.—b. 1912; ed. Tiffin Boys' Sch., Kingston-on-Thames, Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London Univ. and California Inst. of Tech., U.S.A.; interned, 1942-45; geologist, Nyasa., 1935; F.M.S., 1939; prin. geol., Fed. of Mal., 1950; dep. dir., geol. survey, 1957.

ALEXANDER, J. L. N.—b. 1907; ed. Taystock Gram. Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-45, capt. (desps. twice); water and drainage engrnr., P.W.D., Uga., 1946; redesign. chief water and drainage engrnr., 1953.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-45, major; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946; co-op. soc. offr., 1947; registr., co-op. soc., Maur., 1951; comsnr., co-op. devel., Cyp., 1955-60; U.K. delegate, F.A.O./I.L.O.; tech. mtg. on co-ops. in Asia and F.E., 1954; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.
ALEXANDER, W. H.—b. 1907; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca; barrister-at-law; apptd. J'ca, 1927; dep. clk., 1939; clk. of courts, 1947; crown coun., 1950; senr., 1955; resdt. mag., 1955.
ALEXIS, F. C.—b. 1913; ed. Grenada Boys' Secondary Sch. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Edin.; dist. med. offr., St. V., 1935; supt. physician, col. hosp., Grenada, 1952; S.M.O., 1955.

ALFORD, Sir Robert, K.B.E., C.M.G.—b. 1904; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; nav. serv., 1940-45; cadet, Nig., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr. (clk., exec. and leg. couns.), 1938; senr. dist. offr., 1946; fin. sec., Zanz., 1947; ch. sec., 1952; gov. and c-in-c., St. Helena, 1958.
ALI, Ayub, O.B.E. (1956), M.B.E.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Indian Sch., Nairobi; clk., sec't, Ken., 1920; asst. estab. offr., 1944; estab. offr., 1948; asst. sec. (estab.), 1955.

ALKALI, M.—b. 1923; ed. Kaduna Govt. Coll., Samaru and Ibadan Schs. of Agric., and Camb. Univ.; agric. asst-in-training, Nig., 1942; agric. asst., 1945; asst. agric. offr., 1949; agric. supt., 1955; agric. offr., N. Nig., 1958.

ALLAN, A. S., M.B.E.(Mil.) (1943)—b. 1914; ed. Stonyhurst and Univ. of Rennes, France; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; cadet, Tang., 1950; dist. offr., 1952; asst. sec., Ken., 1953; dist. offr., Tang., 1955; estab. offr., 1955; prin. asst. sec., 1959.

ALLAN, C. H., O.B.E. (1959)—b. 1921; ed. Hamilton High Sch., Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z., and Camb. Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-44; cadet, B.S.I.P., 1945; admin. offr., cl. B., 1949; cl. A., 1955; secon., spec. lands comsnr., W. Pac. H.C., 1956; N. Heb., 1958.
ALLAN, D. A. G., M.C.—b. 1918; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1939-47; exec. engrnr., Ken., 1948; senr. exc. engrnr., Nyasa., 1956.

ALLAN, J. A.—b. 1910; ed. Glasgow Univ. and W. of Scotland Agric. Coll.; lectr., Barb., 1936; tech. offr., Nat. Inst. of Agric. Botany, Camb., 1944; supt., agric., Cyprus, 1945; pasture research offr., Ken., 1947; agric. offr., N. Rhod., 1949.
ALLAN, J. A. B.—b. 1911; ed. Douai Sch.; mil. serv., 1940-48 (desps.), lt.-col.; supt., prisons, Mal., 1948; senr. supt., Uga., 1956; dep. comsnr., prisons, 1958.
ALLARDICE, D.—b. 1924; ed. St. Andrews Univ.; mil. serv., 1945-48; cadet engr., E.A.R. and H., 1948; asst. engr., 1950; dist. engr., 1958; resdt. engr., 1959.

ALLCHIN, H. W.—b. 1918; ed. Borough Poly. Sch., London; mil. serv., 1940 and 1942-46; Br. P.O., 1935-50; E.A.P. & T. admin., 1950; techn. gr. I, 1950; inspgr. techn., 1954; asst. engr., gr. I, 1955; senr. asst. engr., 1960.

ALLEN, A. D.—b. 1922; ed. Cheltenham Gram. Sch.; mil. serv., 1941-46; civ. instr., air min., 1947; radio offr., M.C.A., 1948; wireless offr., E.A.H.C. posts and tels. admin., 1949; radio supt., civ. aviation dept., 1954; asst. signals offr., Nig., 1955-61.

ALLEN, C. P.S., M.V.O., O.B.E. (1959)—b. 1917; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Sch., N.Z., and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-43, K.A.R.; admin. cadet, Uga., 1940; asst. dist. offr., 1942; asst. chief sec., 1953; perm. sec. for security and external relations, 1955; secon., Br. embassy, Washington, 1956-57; secon., supvr., elections, Uga., 1958, and Royal tour dir., 1958-59; admin. sec., 1960.

ALLEN, D. J.—b. 1911; ed. Clive Hse. Sch., St. Edward's Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford Univ.; customs prob., F.M.S., 1933; asst. supt., customs and excise, 1936; customs offr., 1939; senr., 1939; contrl., supplies, Aden, 1941-44; asst. compt., customs, Fed. of Mal., 1951.

ALLEN, E. C., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Bradford Commercial Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; clk., sec't, and reporter, leg. co., Nyasa., 1925; asst. treas., Tang., 1930; senr., 1940; secon., as treas., ch. of customs, British Som., 1940; fn. sec. and collr. of inc. tax, Basuto., 1945; treas., 1958-60.
ALLEN, E. W.—b. 1917; ed. London Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-46, major; educ. offr., N. Nig., 1947; senr. educ. offr., 1956; prin., teacher training coll., 1956.
ALLEN, J. W. H.—b. 1913; ed. Regent St. Poly. and Downing Coll., Camb.; asst. audr., Nig., 1936; C.A.D., 1938; Fiji, 1943; senr. audr., Mal., 1948; dep. dir., fed. audit., Nig., 1954; dir., 1958-60.
ALLEN, R. B.—b. 1907; ed. New Fletton Secondary Sch. and County Tech. Coll.; mil. serv., 1940-45, P.O., R.N.; supt. of press, Nig., 1948; dep. govt. printer, 1952; fed. govt. printer, 1958-60.

ALLEN, R. L.—b. 1914; ed. Coombe Down C. of E. Sch. and Bath Tech. Coll.; Br. P.O. 1942-47; Sudan govt. posts and tels. dept., 1945; E.A. posts and tels. dept., techn., gr. I, 1947; insptg. techn., 1949; supvg' techn., 1954; senr. asst. engr., 1955.
ALLEN, S. J. H.—b. 1927; ed. Ardingly Coll.; Pal. police, 1946-48; survr., D.O.S., 1949; staff survr., S. Leone, 1956; asst. dir., survey, 1960.
ALLEY, J. C.—b. 1915; ed. Bishop Foy and Galway Gram. Schs., and Vet. Coll. of Ireland; vet. offr., Nig., 1942-44; Zanz., 1948; Tang., 1952; Aden, 1956; Uga., 1960.

ALLEYNE, K. H. C., Q.C.—b. 1918; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; registr., Dominica, 1943; mag., St. L., 1944; cr. atty., 1949; St. V., 1952; atty.-gen., Grenada, Wind. Is., 1956; author of Memoir of constitutional development of St. Lucia, 1950.

WRIGHT, J. T.—b. 1911; ed. L.C.C. Sch.; mil. serv., 1927-33 and 1941-46; inland rev. dept., 1934; Ken. govt. serv., 1946; E.A.H.C., 1948; asst. estab. offr., E.A. posts and telecoms. admin., 1950; senr. estab. offr., 1955; prin. estab. offr., 1960.
WRIGHT, O. F.—b. 1924; ed. Cheltenham Gram. Sch., and Reading Univ.; agric. offr., Sarawak, 1947; agric. o.in-c., staff training sch., 1954.
WRIGHT, S. V.—b. 1911; ed. Prince of Wales Sch., S.L.; cler. serv., S.L., 1929; asst. col. sec., 1950; clk., house of reps., 1960.
WRIGHT, W. A.—b. 1923; ed. Armagh Academy, Queen's Univ., Belfast, Peterhouse, Camb., and I.C.T.A.; agric. offr., Gam., 1947; Ken., 1950.
WRIGHT, W. J. R.—b. 1911; ed. Falkirk High Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, capt.; admin. cadet, S.L., 1946; asst. dist. comsnr., 1949; clk., ex. co., 1950; dist. comsnr., 1951; admin. offr., gr. II, 1960.
WRIGHT-NOUTH, G. A. R., Q.P.M. (1957) C.P.M.—b. 1917; ed. Bedford Sch.; mil. serv., 1939, lt.; police cadet, H.K., 1939; asst. supt., 1942; senr. supt., 1952; asst. comsnr., 1953.
WYILE, H. W.—b. 1923; ed. Stockport Gram. Sch. and Manchester Univ.; M.O., N. Borneo, 1947; H.K., 1954; prin. med. and health offr., 1960.
WYLIE, A. D. B., O.B.E. (1957)—b. 1909; ed. Leith Acad.; mil. serv., 1939-45; press supt., Tang., 1949; asst. govt. printer, 1952; govt. printer, 1953.

WYLIE, C., Q.C., E.D.—b. 1905; ed. Auckland Gram. Sch. and N.Z. Univ.; barrister, Inner Temple; mil. serv., 1941-46, maj. (desps.); cr. coun., Mal., 1946; senr. fedl. coun., 1950; atty.-gen., Barb., 1951; B. Guiana, 1955; atty.-gen., Br. Caribbean pre-fed. estab., 1956; W. Indies, 1958; fed. justice, 1959.

WYN-HARRIS, Sir Percy, K.C.M.G. (1950), M.B.E.—b. 1903; ed. Gresham's Sch., Holt, and Caius Coll., Camb.; cadet, Ken., 1926; settlement offr. (Kikuyu land claims), 1939-40; lab. liaison offr., 1944; prov. comsnr., 1945; ch. native comsnr., 1947; gov. and c-in-c., Gam., 1949-58; apptd. admnr., N. Cameroons, 1960.
XUEREB, I. M.—b. 1910; ed. Lyceum, and Royal Univ., Malta; 2nd cl. postal clk., Malta, 1930; postal clk., 1947; spec. cl., 1948; acctnt., posts and tel. dept., 1959.
XUEREB, J. L.—b. 1906; ed. Lyceum and Royal Univ. of Malta; postal clk., Malta, 1925; sub. inspr., police, 1928; inspr., 1936; supt., 1941; asst. comsnr., police, 1954; dep. comsnr., 1956.

YATES, A. J., C.P.M. (1961).—b. 1914; ed. Ashford Gram. Sch., and Royal Agric. Coll., Cirencester; mil. serv., 1939–42; vet. dept., Nyasa., 1939–43; asst. inspr., police, 1943; asst. supt., 1948; supt., 1954; senr., 1960.
YEARWOOD, R. K.—b. 1918; ed. Dulwich Coll.; mil. serv., 1939–46; capt.; customs cadet, Mal., 1946; senr. customs offr., S'pore, 1953; asst. comsnr., customs, N. Borneo, 1957.

YELDHAM, R. E. S., C.B.E. (1945).—b. 1902; ed. St. Bees Sch. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; mil. serv., 1923–46; colonel; civil comsnr., Maur., 1947–49; secon., C.O., 1948; Mal. C.S. (secon. C.O.), 1952; asst. sec., Ken., 1954; contlr., supp. and transport, 1955–60.
YELF, J. D.—b. 1920; ed. Taunton Sch., Trinity Hall, Camb. and I.C.T.A.; agric. offr., N. Rhod., 1943; senr. agric. offr., Fiji, 1956.

YEO, D.—b. 1925; ed. Univ. Coll., Exeter; scientific offr., Uga., 1948; Tang., 1950; senr. scientific offr., 1951; prin. scientific offr., physicist to col. pesticides research unit, Tang., 1956; publ. Aircraft Applications of Insecticides in E. Africa; and The Application of Agricultural Chemicals from Aircraft in the U.S.A.
YEOMAN, G. H.—b. 1920; ed. Highgate Sch. and Royal Vet. Coll., London; mil. serv., 1939–45, lieut.; vet. offr., Tang., 1949.
YONGE, D. D., M.C.—b. 1918; ed. Epsom Coll., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939–46, capt.; cadet, Tang., 1948; dist. offr., 1950; admin. offr., cl. IIB, 1959.
YONGE, Miss E. M.—ed. St. Hugh's Coll., Oxford; woman educ. offr., Ken., 1947.

YORK, A. D.—b. 1907; ed. The King's Hospital and Trinity Coll., Dublin; interned 1942–45; cadet, M.C.S., 1929; cl. V, 1932; res., Labuan, 1934; cl. IV, 1936; D.O., 1938; cl. III, 1941; cl. II, 1946; res. comsnr., cl. IB, 1948; p.a.s., comsnr-gen., S.E. Asia, 1950; pres., munic. coun., K. Lumpur, cl. IA, 1954.
YORKE, F. G.—b. 1914; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. L.; labr. inspr., St. L., 1952; prin. clk., admin., 1955; supt., prisons, staff offr., spec. reserve, police, 1958.

YOUENS, P. W., O.B.E. (1960).—b. 1916; ed. King Edward VII Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; naval serv. 1939–40, sub-lt.; cadet, S.L., 1939; asst. dist. comsnr., 1942; dist. comsnr., 1948; com. H.Q. judicial and Freetown police dists., 1950; asst. sec., Nyasa., 1951; dep. ch. sec., 1953; M.L.C., 1950 and 1954.

YOUNG, A. J.—b. 1909; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin.; clk., post office and savings bank, Trin., 1930–44; senr. clk., sect', 1944; prin. offr., cl. I, 1949; asst. sec., 1952; senr. asst. sec., 1957–60.
YOUNG, G. S.—b. 1925; ed. City of Oxford High Sch., and Reading Univ.; tsset offr., Tang., 1949.

YOUNG, G. V. C.—b. 1921; ed. Cabin Hill, Knock, Belfast, Campbell Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; barrister-at-law, N. Ireland; crown coun., G.C., 1947; asst. atty-gen., Sarawak, 1955; dep. public prosecutor, 1955.
YOUNG, J.—b. 1913; ed. R.N. Coll., Dartmouth, Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; cadet, Tang., 1936; dist. offr., 1938; admin. offr., cl. IIB, 1958.

YOUNG, J. A. E.—b. 1912; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana and B.N.C., Oxford; temp. cler. asst., local govt. bd., B. Guiana, 1930; survr's apprentice, 1932; cl. III clk., 1935; survr., 1938; dist. comsnr., 1947; prin. asst. sec., 1954; perm. sec., 1957; publ. The Approaches to local Self Government in B. Guiana (1958); Some Milestones in Village History (1956); etc.
YOUNG, J. C.—b. 1914; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; prob. audit dept., B. Guiana, 1935; clk., 1936–50; senr. examining offr., 1950; audr., 1954; senr. (later prin.) audr., 1957.

YOUNG, K. D., O.B.E. (1961), Brill. Star of Zanz.—b. 1916; ed. Sherborne, Trinity Hall, Camb., and St. Geo. Hosp., London; mil. serv., 1940–46, surg. lt.-com.; M.O., Zanz., 1947; S.M.O. (clinical), Som., 1955.

YOUNG, R. H.—b. 1912; ed. Highgate Sch.; mil. serv., 1939–46, major; apptd. information dept., C.O., 1948; asst. public relations advr., D.W.W.I., 1951–53; public relations advr., B. Guiana, 1953–54; senr. inf. offr., C.O., 1954; prin. U.K. inf. offr., Tang., 1958–59; prin. inf. offr. C.O., 1960.

YOUNG, W. M., C.B.E.—b. 1917; ed. Wellington Coll. and Camb. Univ.; interned, 1942–45; cadet, M.C.S., 1939; cl. V, 1942 (retros.); cl. IV, 1946; asst. comsnr., labr., Mal., 1948; cl. III, 1950; dep. comsnr., labr., Fed. of Mal., 1955; sec., external def., (min of labr., etc. later) 1956; superscale D, 1957; ret. 1960.

YOUNGER, W. M., M.B.E. (Mil.) (1941).—b. 1906; ed. Boys' High Sch., Pretoria and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; mil. serv., 1939–45, maj.; survey dept. N. Rhod., 1930; survr., 1939; senr., 1946; ch. survr., 1955; comsnr., lands, 1957.
YOUNGJOHNS, B. J.—b. 1924; ed. King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1942–47; asst. registr., co-op. socs., Basuto., 1952; registr., 1957.

YOUNGLAO, L. P.—b. 1901; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin., Univ. Coll., Dublin, L.S.H.T.M. and Johns Hopkins Univ., U.S.A.; M.O., Dominica, 1930; S.M.O. and M.O.H., 1945; S.M.O., B. Hond., 1948; M.O., Trin., 1950; D.D.M.S., (preventive), 1955.
YOUNIE, E. M.—b. 1926; ed. Fettes Coll., and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1944–46; cadet, Tang., 1950; dist. offr., 1952.
ZAMMIT, C. G.—b. 1911; ed. Lyceum, Malta and Univ. of Lond. Inst. of Archaeol.; asst. curator, archaeol., 1930; curator, 1934; dir., museum, Malta, 1955.

ZANS, V. A.—b. 1904; ed. Riga Univ.; govt. geologist, (later dir., geol. surveys) J’ca, 1949; publ. Economic Geology and Mineral Resources of J’ca (Bull. No. 1 Geol. Surv. Dept., 1951); On Karst Hydrology in J’ca (Assn. Internationale d’Hydrologie Scientifique—Bruxelles 1951—Extr. Tome II, 1951); Manganese Deposits of Marshall’s Hall, J’ca and Bauxite Resources of J’ca and their Development (Col. Geol. Min. Res., Vol. 3, Nos. 2 and 4, 1952).
ZEHNDER, L. P.—b. 1909; ed. St., Thomas’ Sch., Sarawak, Bishop Cotton Sch., Bangalore, and Univ. Coll., Rangoon; asst. forest offr., Sarawak, 1946; asst. consvr., forests, 1951.
ZEPHIRIN, Miss M. P.—b. 1930; ed. General Hosp., Boston, Mass., U.S.A.; nutrition offr., Wind. Is., 1956.
